title: rubus tarnensis
intro: Rubus tarnensis is a lesser-known species of bramble, closely related to the raspberry and blackberry plants, and is native to the mountainous regions of Tarn, France. The medicinal properties of Rubus tarnensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which may help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of this plant may include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Rubus tarnensis can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
